title: elymus elongatus subsp. ponticus
intro: Elymus elongatus subsp. ponticus is a subspecies of the tall fescue grass, a type of wild grass native to the Pontic Mountains of Eurasia. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Elymus elongatus subsp. ponticus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and support the immune system. The herbal preparations of this plant are usually made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is also used in traditional medicine in the form of decoctions and infusions.
